parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: In your English class you have been talking about transport in cities. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay. Write an essay using all the notes and giving reasons for your point of view.

stimulus.body:
"The best way to reduce traffic in cities is to make public transport free for everyone. Do you agree?"

Notes

Write about:
1. cost
2. convenience
3. ............... (your own idea)

bullets: give reasons for your point of view

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement on an English-language website for travellers.

stimulus.title: Rate your local transport
stimulus.body: We want honest reviews of buses, trains, trams and metro lines from the people who actually use them. Write a review of a service you use regularly, describing a typical journey, mentioning what is good and bad about it, and saying whether a visitor to your area should use it.

bullets: what is good and bad about the service
whether a visitor should use it

tasks.lead: Your town council is reviewing local bus services and has asked students at your school to write reports.

stimulus.title: Report: how students travel to school
stimulus.body: Describe how students at your school usually travel to school, explain why many of them do not use the bus, and recommend two changes that would encourage more students to travel by bus.

bullets: how students usually travel to school
why many do not use the bus
two changes to encourage bus travel

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Chris.

stimulus.body:
From: Chris
Subject: Getting around

I've booked my visit at last! One thing, though — I won't have a car, and I'll be staying quite far from the centre. How easy is it to get around your town without driving? What's the cheapest way for me to travel every day, and is there anything I should watch out for, like strikes or crowded times?
See you soon,
Chris

bullets: how easy it is to get around without a car
the cheapest way to travel every day
something Chris should watch out for
